reasoning: The spread as of Feb 13 is 0.64, which is near the bottom of its recent 1-month range (0.62 to 0.74). The cooler-than-expected CPI print on Feb 13 helped the spread bounce from 0.62 to 0.64, as lower inflation expectations typically drive the shorter end of the curve down faster (bull steepening). Because the current starting value (0.64) is relatively low compared to the Jan-Feb average (~0.70) and structurally the curve is expected to steepen in a Fed cutting environment, there is a strong historical/statistical probability of a bounce back above 0.64. Therefore, I favor an increase. As requested, my probability moderates closer to 0.5 for the second horizon due to increased uncertainty (e.g., upcoming March economic data like NFP and another CPI report).
